The pySEs public API
====================

All user-facing functionality is exposed through a small set of Python
modules in the ``src/`` directory.  They are imported as, e.g.,
``from pyses.grids import init``.  Internal implementation details live
in the sub-packages below ``src/`` and are not part of the stable API.

.. contents:: Modules
   :local:
   :depth: 1

----

``pyses.grids`` — Horizontal grid generation
-----------------------------------------------

Provides two namespaces for building and manipulating the spectral-element
horizontal grid.

.. code-block:: python

    from pyses.grids import init, parallelism

``init`` — grid constructors
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

All constructors return ``(h_grid, dims)``, where ``h_grid`` is the
spectral-element grid struct and ``dims`` is a metadata dict used throughout
the rest of the API.

``init.init_quasi_uniform_grid(nx, npt, ...)``
    Element-local quasi-uniform cubed-sphere.  This is the recommended
    constructor for most production runs; it avoids the coordinate singularities
    of the equiangular metric.

``init.init_equiangular_grid(nx, npt, ...)``
    Equiangular cubed-sphere using a global metric tensor.  Useful when the
    equiangular property is desirable (e.g. certain convergence tests).

``init.init_stretched_grid(nx, npt, axis_dilation, ...)``
    Element-local stretched cubed-sphere.  ``axis_dilation`` is a length-3
    array that dilates the cube along each Cartesian axis, concentrating
    resolution near the two antipodal points where the axis intersects the
    sphere.

``init.init_unstructured_grid(...)``
    Build a grid from an externally supplied unstructured mesh (e.g. one
    generated by SCRIP or Exodus).

``init.init_periodic_plane_grid(nx, npt, ...)``
    Doubly-periodic flat-plane grid for idealised channel experiments.

``init.exodus_to_pyses_grid_corners(filepath)``
    Read an Exodus II mesh file and convert its corner-node coordinates into
    the format expected by ``init_unstructured_grid``.

``parallelism`` — distributed-memory helpers
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

``parallelism.shard_grid(h_grid, dims)``
    Distribute ``h_grid`` across MPI ranks or JAX devices.

``parallelism.get_global_grid(h_grid, dims)``
    Gather a sharded grid back onto the root rank/device.

``parallelism.make_grid_mpi_ready(h_grid, dims)``
    Prepare a locally-built grid for use in an MPI run (adds halo metadata).

----

``pyses.initialize`` — Initial conditions
-------------------------------------------

.. code-block:: python

    from pyses.initialize import ullrich_baroclinic_wave, custom_init

``ullrich_baroclinic_wave`` — DCMIP 2016 moist baroclinic wave
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Implements the Ullrich–Waite–Jablonowski moist baroclinic-wave test case
(`Ullrich et al. 2014 <https://doi.org/10.1002/qj.2241>`_), which is the
standard benchmark for 3-D dynamical cores in pySEs.

``ullrich_baroclinic_wave.init_baroclinic_wave_config(model_config)``
    Derive the test-case configuration (analytic profiles, planetary constants)
    from the physics config dict.  Returns a ``test_config`` dict consumed by
    ``init_baroclinic_wave_state``.

``ullrich_baroclinic_wave.init_baroclinic_wave_state(h_grid, v_grid, physics_config, test_config, dims, model, mountain=False, pert_type=...)``
    Evaluate the analytic initial state on the grid and return a full model
    state dict.  Key options:

    * ``mountain`` — add an Agnesi-profile mountain to trigger orographic wave
      growth.
    * ``pert_type`` — one of ``ullrich_baroclinic_wave.perturbation_opts``:

      * ``perturbation_opts.streamfunction`` — the perturbation form from the
        original paper (default).
      * ``perturbation_opts.exponential`` — DCMIP 2016 exponential form.
      * ``perturbation_opts.none`` — no explicit perturbation; the wave grows
        from numerical noise (useful with topography).

``ullrich_baroclinic_wave.perturbation_opts``
    Enum of perturbation types described above.

``custom_init`` — low-level initialisation primitives
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

For users building custom initial conditions rather than using an analytic
test case.

``custom_init.init_model_pressure(...)``
    Compute the initial pressure profile given a surface-pressure field.

``custom_init.init_static_forcing(phi_surf, h_grid, physics_config, dims, model)``
    Compute the time-invariant ``static_forcing`` dict (surface geopotential
    gradient, Coriolis parameter) from a surface geopotential array.

``custom_init.init_model_struct_homme(wind, theta_v_d_mass, d_mass, phi_surf, moisture_species, tracers, h_grid, dims, config, model, ...)``
    Assemble a complete model state for HOMME-family dynamical cores.

``custom_init.init_model_struct_cam_se(wind, T, d_mass, phi_surf, moisture_species, tracers, h_grid, dims, config, model, ...)``
    Assemble a complete model state for CAM-SE-family dynamical cores.

----

``pyses.model_utils`` — Model configuration and utilities
-----------------------------------------------------------

.. code-block:: python

    from pyses.model_utils import (
        mass_coordinate, model_config, model_info,
        model_state, parallel_utils, operators,
    )

``model_info`` — model identifiers
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

``model_info.models``
    Enum of all available dynamical cores:

    * ``models.homme_hydrostatic`` — hydrostatic HOMME (spherical)
    * ``models.homme_nonhydrostatic`` — non-hydrostatic HOMME (spherical)
    * ``models.homme_nonhydrostatic_deep`` — non-hydrostatic HOMME, deep
      atmosphere (full Coriolis vector)
    * ``models.homme_hydrostatic_f_plane`` — hydrostatic HOMME, f-plane
    * ``models.homme_nonhydrostatic_f_plane`` — non-hydrostatic HOMME, f-plane
    * ``models.cam_se`` — CAM-SE hydrostatic
    * ``models.cam_se_whole_atmosphere`` — CAM-SE with variable gas constant
      for whole-atmosphere runs

``model_config`` — physics and numerics configuration
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

``model_config.init_default_config(nx, h_grid, v_grid, dims, model, hypervis_type=...)``
    Convenience function that returns sensible defaults for all three
    configuration dicts needed by the simulator:
    ``(physics_config, diffusion_config, timestep_config)``.
    The returned dicts are plain Python dicts and can be modified in-place
    before being passed to the simulator.

``model_config.hypervis_opts``
    Enum controlling the hyperviscosity scaling law:

    * ``hypervis_opts.variable_resolution`` — scales with local element size;
      correct for stretched and unstructured grids.  Safe to use on uniform
      grids too.
    * ``hypervis_opts.quasi_uniform`` — assumes all elements are the same size;
      slightly cheaper on uniform grids.
    * ``hypervis_opts.none`` — no hyperviscosity (unstable without another
      stabilisation mechanism).

``model_config.init_physics_config(...)``
    Build the physics config dict directly, with full control over planetary
    constants, gas constants, and moisture species parameters.

``model_config.init_timestep_config(...)``
    Build the timestep config dict, setting physics and dynamics timesteps,
    subcycle counts, and hyperviscosity substep counts independently.

``model_config.init_diffusion_config(...)``
    Build the diffusion (hyperviscosity + sponge-layer) config dict.

``mass_coordinate`` — vertical grid utilities
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

``mass_coordinate.init_vertical_grid(hybrid_a_i, hybrid_b_i, p0, model)``
    Construct the vertical grid struct from hybrid sigma-pressure interface
    coefficients.  ``p0`` is the reference surface pressure in Pa.

``mass_coordinate.surface_mass_to_interface_mass(ps, v_grid)``
    Convert surface pressure to interface-level pressure.

``mass_coordinate.surface_mass_to_midlevel_mass(ps, v_grid)``
    Convert surface pressure to mid-level pressure.

``mass_coordinate.d_mass_to_surface_mass(d_mass, v_grid)``
    Recover surface pressure from a ``d_mass`` array by summing over levels.

``model_state`` — state arithmetic and remapping
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

These functions operate on the model state dicts described in
:doc:`anatomy_of_a_model_struct`.

``model_state.sum_dynamics(states, coeffs, model)``
    Weighted linear combination of a list of dynamics state dicts (used by
    the Runge–Kutta integrator).

``model_state.sum_tracers(tracer_states, coeffs, model)``
    Same weighted combination for tracer state dicts.

``model_state.remap_dynamics(dynamics, static_forcing, v_grid, physics_config, num_lev, model)``
    Conservatively remap all dynamics fields onto the reference hybrid-
    coordinate levels using the Zerroukat monotone remapper.

``model_state.remap_tracers(dynamics, tracers, v_grid, num_lev, model)``
    Same conservative remap for tracer fields.

``model_state.wrap_dynamics / wrap_tracers / wrap_static_forcing / wrap_model_state``
    Low-level constructors that assemble the sub-dicts of a model state.
    Prefer ``custom_init.init_model_struct_*`` for full initialisation.

``model_state.copy_dynamics / copy_tracers / copy_model_state``
    Deep-copy helpers that allocate independent arrays (needed before
    in-place modification).

``model_state.check_dynamics_nan / check_tracers_nan``
    Return ``True`` if any field contains a NaN; ghost/halo nodes are excluded
    before the check, and the result is reduced across all MPI ranks.

``parallel_utils`` — array device helpers
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

``parallel_utils.device_wrapper(x)``
    Move a NumPy array onto the compute device (no-op for NumPy back-end;
    creates a JAX array for the JAX back-end).

``parallel_utils.device_unwrapper(x)``
    Retrieve a device array as a NumPy array.

``parallel_utils.get_global_array(x, dims)``
    Gather a (potentially sharded) array and strip padding elements,
    returning a plain NumPy array with shape ``(n_elem, npt, npt, ...)``.

``operators`` — 3-D differential operators
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

All operators accept a field of shape ``(n_elem, npt, npt, n_lev)`` and
return the expected output shape.

``operators.horizontal_gradient_3d(scalar, h_grid, dims)``
    Horizontal gradient of a scalar; returns shape ``(n_elem, npt, npt, n_lev, 2)``.

``operators.horizontal_divergence_3d(vector, h_grid, dims)``
    Horizontal divergence of a vector field.

``operators.horizontal_vorticity_3d(vector, h_grid, dims)``
    Horizontal vorticity (curl) of a vector field.

``operators.horizontal_weak_laplacian_3d(scalar, h_grid, dims)``
    Weak-form scalar Laplacian.

``operators.horizontal_weak_vector_laplacian_3d(vector, h_grid, dims)``
    Weak-form vector Laplacian.

``operators.project_scalar_3d(scalar, h_grid, dims)``
    DSS (Direct Stiffness Summation) projection to enforce C0
    continuity across element boundaries.

----

``pyses.simulate`` — Running the dynamical core
-------------------------------------------------

.. code-block:: python

    from pyses.simulate import init_simulator, advance_coupling_step

``init_simulator(h_grid, v_grid, physics_config, diffusion_config, timestep_config, dims, model)``
    Build a Python generator that advances the model forward in time
    indefinitely.  Call it with an initial state to obtain a generator
    object, then iterate:

    .. code-block:: python

        simulator = init_simulator(h_grid, v_grid, physics_config,
                                   diffusion_config, timestep_config,
                                   dims, model)
        for t, state in simulator(initial_state):
            if t > end_time:
                break

    To inject physics tendencies, use ``generator.send(physics_forcing)``
    instead of iterating directly.

``advance_coupling_step(state_in, h_grid, v_grid, physics_config, diffusion_config, timestep_config, dims, model, physics_forcing=None)``
    Advance the model by exactly **one physics timestep**.  This is the
    lower-level counterpart of the generator loop: it performs subcycled
    dynamics, tracer advection with consistency corrections, sponge-layer
    damping, and vertical remapping, then returns the updated state dict.

    Use this directly when you need explicit control over the time loop
    (e.g. coupling to an external physics package that manages its own
    outer loop).

----

``pyses.shallow_water`` — Shallow-water models
------------------------------------------------

A self-contained set of 2-D shallow-water solvers on the sphere, useful for
testing the horizontal discretisation in isolation.

.. code-block:: python

    from pyses.shallow_water import (
        simulate_shallow_water,
        configure,
        model_state,
        galewsky_init,
        williamson_init,
    )

``simulate_shallow_water(h_grid, model_state, config, hv_config, timestep_config, dims, ...)``
    Run the shallow-water model for a prescribed number of steps and return
    the final state.

``configure``
    Configuration helpers analogous to those in ``model_utils.model_config``:

    * ``configure.init_hypervis_config_quasi_uniform(...)`` — constant-coefficient hyperviscosity for uniform grids.
    * ``configure.init_hypervis_config_variable_res(...)`` — tensor hyperviscosity scaled by local element size for stretched grids.
    * ``configure.init_timestep_config(...)`` — timestep and subcycle counts.

``model_state``
    * ``model_state.wrap_model_state(height, wind)`` — assemble a shallow-water state dict.
    * ``model_state.project_model_state(state, h_grid, dims)`` — DSS-project all fields.

``williamson_init`` — Williamson test cases
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Standard shallow-water test suite (`Williamson et al. 1992
<https://doi.org/10.1016/S0021-9991(05)80016-6>`_).

* ``williamson_init.init_williamson_steady_config(...)`` — planetary constants for Williamson Test Case 2.
* ``williamson_init.init_williamson_tc2_wind(lat, lon, config)`` — analytic wind field.
* ``williamson_init.init_williamson_tc2_h(lat, lon, config)`` — analytic height field.
* ``williamson_init.init_williamson_tc2_hs(lat, lon, config)`` — analytic surface geopotential.

``galewsky_init`` — Galewsky jet test case
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Mid-latitude jet instability test (`Galewsky et al. 2004
<https://doi.org/10.3402/tellusa.v56i5.14436>`_).

* ``galewsky_init.init_galewsky_config(...)`` — planetary constants and jet parameters.
* ``galewsky_init.eval_galewky_wind(lat, config)`` — analytic zonal wind.
* ``galewsky_init.eval_galewky_h(lat, config)`` — analytic height field.
* ``galewsky_init.eval_galewky_hs(lat, config)`` — analytic surface height.
